Event: Rip apologizes to Lloyd before beating him senseless
Explanation: He gains no clear benefit and seriously harms a loyal colleague over a petty grudge issue.

Event: Rip beats Lloyd senseless
Explanation: Violent dominance may reinforce his authority but badly harms Lloyd physically and relationally.

Event: Rip administers beatdowns to wranglers who cross a line
Explanation: He harms offenders but maintains overall ranch order and his own authority.

Event: Rip intentionally loses a fight to Kayce so wranglers accept Kayce as boss
Explanation: He sacrifices his dominance to strengthen Kayce’s leadership and ranch stability.

Event: Rip shows up to rescue Beth from a kill squad
Explanation: He’s badly injured but successfully saves Beth’s life and stops the attackers.

Event: Rip continues fighting and gets shot twice while rescuing Beth
Explanation: He incurs serious physical cost yet preserves Beth and thwarts the hit squad.

Event: Rip refrains from getting close to Beth despite loving her
Explanation: Holding back denies himself happiness and offers Beth little clear benefit.

Event: Rip is initially taken aback at Beth dating other men but does nothing
Explanation: He endures emotional pain without changing circumstances for himself or others.

Event: Rip rescues Beth from Beck’s men (triggering his confession)
Explanation: He risks himself to save her and this also advances their relationship.

Event: Rip finally admits his feelings to Beth after rescuing her
Explanation: Honesty deepens mutual emotional fulfillment with no shown downside.

Event: Rip ruthlessly protects the ranch from thieves and intruders
Explanation: He secures his home and position while harshly harming adversaries.

Event: Rip shrugs off serious injuries to keep working for the ranch
Explanation: He endangers his health but maintains protection and continuity for everyone.

Event: Rip serves as John’s right-hand man, doing whatever it takes to protect the ranch
Explanation: He gains status and belonging while using lethal methods against threats.

Event: Rip kills people in cold blood at John’s request
Explanation: He strengthens his and John’s security by severely harming or eliminating others.

Event: Rip lets Kayce win their fight
Explanation: He sacrifices personal reputation so Kayce gains authority and the ranch gains cohesion.

Event: Rip goes to expense to give his mother a proper burial
Explanation: He spends resources for emotional closure and to honor her, no visible harm caused.

Event: Rip pays a private gravedigger for his mother’s burial
Explanation: He loses money but gains dignity and provides income to the gravedigger.

Event: Rip has his mother’s body exhumed to retrieve her ring
Explanation: He gets a sentimental ring but disturbs her grave, emotional gains offset by desecration.

Event: Rip speaks apologetically to his mother’s body and shares news of Beth/marriage proposal
Explanation: He gains emotional comfort and symbolic connection without hurting anyone.

Event: Rip stops John from beating Jamie
Explanation: He risks John’s displeasure to protect Jamie from further abuse.

Event: Much later, Rip tells off John for how he treats Jamie
Explanation: He risks conflict with his patron to advocate for fairer treatment of Jamie.

Event: Rip routinely snaps at Jimmy, telling him to “shut the fuck up”
Explanation: He vents frustration and asserts dominance while belittling Jimmy.

Event: Rip snaps at Lloyd in Season 4
Explanation: He reinforces his authority at the cost of their relationship and Lloyd’s dignity.

Event: Rip chooses to remain essentially John’s lifelong “slave”
Explanation: He gains belonging and purpose but accepts structural exploitation and limited autonomy.

Event: Rip maintains absolute loyalty to John partly because he had nowhere else to go
Explanation: His loyalty secures shelter and status but stems from lack of options and dependence.

Event: Rip melts and becomes gentle around Beth
Explanation: His softness enriches their bond and harms no one.

Event: Rip melts and becomes gentle around children
Explanation: His kindness benefits kids and emotionally benefits him.

Event: Rip accidentally kills someone in a fight in a flashback
Explanation: The killing harms the victim yet binds Rip to the ranch with long‑term security.

Event: Rip comments on his bullet wounds: “I was built to go through hell” and underreacts
Explanation: Minimizing injury keeps operations moving but endangers his long‑term health.

Event: Rip remains uneasy during his courtship and wedding with Beth
Explanation: His vulnerability and awkwardness deepen their authentic relationship.

Event: Rip admits his fears about marriage to Lloyd
Explanation: Sharing fears gives him support and strengthens trust with Lloyd.

Event: Rip is touched when Jamie says he thinks of him as a friend
Explanation: He gains emotional affirmation and a more positive bond with Jamie.

Event: Rip stands up for Jamie over John’s abuse, reminding John Jamie is still his son
Explanation: He risks disfavor to protect Jamie and prompt John toward better behavior.

Event: As a child, Rip kills his father after his father murders the rest of the family
Explanation: He stops an immediate threat and survives but commits severe violence with long‑term costs.

Event: Rip becomes the ranch’s enforcer, keeping wranglers in line
Explanation: He gains power and security while using coercion and fear on others.

Event: Rip has no problem killing people in cold blood for ranch operations
Explanation: He protects the ranch and his status at the extreme expense of others’ lives.

Event: Rip extends his “will do anything” loyalty to Beth
Explanation: Total commitment benefits their relationship and her security with minimal downside shown.

Event: Rip marries Beth and promises to protect her for life
Explanation: Marriage and vow provide mutual emotional and practical security.

Event: Rip continually seeks John’s approval since youth
Explanation: He gains some validation but remains dependent on John’s judgment.

Event: Rip accepts a demotion to rank‑and‑file wrangler so Kayce can lead
Explanation: He sacrifices status and power so Kayce gains leadership experience.

Event: John rewards Rip with a house of his own
Explanation: Rip gains major material benefit and John rewards proven loyalty, strengthening their bond.
